mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in PAYEMS (nonfarm payrolls) directly signal worsening labor market conditions, which reduce consumer income and spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS increases or remains flat over consecutive months but SPX 20-day returns are positive more than 60% of the time, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate at or below 0.5% indicates the Fed has exhausted conventional easing and will likely implement unconventional stimulus (QE), which boosts equity prices over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: If after FEDFUNDS reaches <=0.5% the SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of cases, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1,000,000 units (HOUST < 1000) indicate a deeply depressed housing sector, which historically marks the bottom of the housing cycle; subsequent recovery leads to positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of periods when HOUST < 1000, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Three consecutive months of negative month-over-month growth in INDPRO signals a severe industrial contraction, leading to negative forward 5-day returns as earnings expectations collapse.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change negative for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: If 5-day SPX returns are positive following three consecutive negative INDPRO months in more than 30% of cases, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in both PAYEMS (employment) and RSAFS (retail sales) indicate a synchronized demand contraction, which is more reliably negative for equities than either signal alone. The interaction amplifies the negative return prediction over 20 days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS and RSAFS both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: If the joint occurrence of declines in both PAYEMS and RSAFS does not produce negative SPX 20-day returns at least 70% of the time, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeds 6% and is rising month-over-month, the labor market deterioration dominates positive sentiment from policy expectations, resulting in negative forward 60-day returns.
failure_condition: If during the regime (UNRATE>6% and rising) SPX 60-day returns are positive more than 40% of the time, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP (GDPC1) confirms the economy is in contraction, and forward 60-day returns are negative as earnings deteriorate.
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day returns are positive following a negative GDP quarter in more than 30% of cases, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the Fed funds rate is at the zero bound (≤0.25%) and industrial production is declining month-over-month, the economy faces a liquidity trap with supply contraction, making equities highly risky; forward 20-day returns are negative.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 30% of instances when both conditions hold, reject.
